title: Fix broken button/border corners across shared components
description:
The 1.5px green outline on the ghost buttons renders with broken/notched corners — the border shows square corners poking past the rounded clip. User first reported the home screen 'Add a plant' and 'Check in' buttons; then the same defect on the 'Add a new room' button in the 'Where do they live?' add-plant sheet. Asked for a general check-in on ALL shared components to make sure every corner renders correctly.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): the anti-pattern .border(color, width:) followed by .cornerRadius(r). SwiftUI's .border draws a rectangular (square-cornered) stroke; the later .cornerRadius() clips the view to a rounded shape, which slices off the straight border at each corner — leaving the corners looking wrong. The fix is to stroke a rounded shape instead, e.g. .overlay(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: r, style: .continuous).stroke(color, lineWidth: width)) (and clip any background separately), rather than .border(...).cornerRadius(...).

BOTH reported cases come from ONE shared component: SproutGhostButtonStyle in Sources/DesignSystem/Buttons.swift:24 (the offending lines are .border(...) at 33-36 then .cornerRadius(18) at 37). It's used in Sources/Views/HomeView.swift:156 and :169 (Add a plant / Check in) and Sources/Views/AddFlowView.swift:194 (Add a new room) — so fixing the style fixes all three usages at once.

GENERAL CHECK-IN RESULT: grepping Sources/ for '.border(' finds only TWO occurrences — SproutGhostButtonStyle (above) and one more one-off at Sources/Views/PlantEditView.swift:165 (same .border(...).cornerRadius(...) pattern, likely the same visual bug on the Edit Plant form). No other shared component uses .border; the other button styles (SproutCreamButtonStyle, primary button) and cards/chips already use .cornerRadius on a filled background or an .overlay(RoundedRectangle...) stroke, so they're not affected. So the corner-rendering sweep is small: fix SproutGhostButtonStyle once, plus the PlantEditView:165 one-off, and both reported symptoms plus any latent one are resolved.

Why it matters: it's a visible polish defect on primary CTAs (home add/check-in) and in the onboarding add-plant flow — cheap, contained fix with outsized visual payoff.

title: Expose sun/humidity levers for preset rooms in Add Room
description:
When adding a new room via the Add Room sheet (Sources/Views/AddRoomView.swift), a preset room type (Living Room, Kitchen, Bedroom, Bathroom, etc.) only shows a read-only 'TYPICAL SETTINGS' card with two static chips — Brightness (e.g. Bright) and Humidity (e.g. Normal) — with NO way to adjust them. The editable Direct Sun / Indirect Sun / Humidity segmented controls only appear when you pick 'Other…' (the CUSTOM ROOM path). User's point: rooms of the same type vary a lot — we don't know what every living room looks like (some are brighter/darker/more humid than others) — so the direct-sun / indirect-sun / humidity levers should be available on EVERY room-type option, with the chosen preset simply setting them as editable DEFAULTS that the user can then override before saving.

CONTEXT / CODE ANCHORS (the machinery already exists — this is mostly surfacing it):
- AddRoomView already stores @State directSun / indirectSun / humidity (Sources/Views/AddRoomView.swift:17-19) and already ADOPTS the selected preset's defaults into that state when a preset is chosen (adoptPresetDefaults, ~lines 133-139: directSun = preset.directSun; indirectSun = preset.indirectSun; humidity = preset.humidity). So Save already uses the preset's values even today.
- The branch that hides them is ~lines 101-105: for a preset it renders presetSummarySection(preset) — the read-only TYPICAL SETTINGS chips (~line 144) — and only the 'Other' path shows the editable segmented controls.
- The exact editable segmented lever UI (Direct Sun Low/Medium/High, Indirect Sun Low/Medium/High, Humidity Dry/Normal/Moist, each with an ⓘ info popover) already exists in two places to reuse: the 'Other' custom path in AddRoomView, and RoomEditorView (Sources/Views/RoomsView.swift:121) which the EDIT-room flow uses. So the same controls that edit an existing room just need to show on the preset path too.
- Preset default values come from RoomPreset.common (Sources/Model/RoomPreset.swift); Brightness is inferred from directSun+indirectSun (Brightness.inferred), and the schedule factor from RoomEnvironment.factor(brightness,humidity).

So the essence: on the preset path, pre-fill the three levers from the preset and show the SAME editable segmented controls (instead of / in addition to the read-only TYPICAL SETTINGS chips), so a user can fine-tune before saving rather than having to save then go edit the room afterward. (Related: the current footer copy even says 'fine-tune it later by editing the room' — this idea lets them fine-tune up front.)

Why it matters: preset light/humidity are guesses; letting the user correct them at creation makes the watering schedule accurate from day one instead of requiring a follow-up edit.

title: Randomise button should also reroll token colour + glyph
description:
On the Add Plants basket (Sources/Views/AddFlowView.swift), each basket entry row (e.g. Stella / Scarlett) has a shuffle/'random' button. Today it ONLY rerolls the plant's nickname — user wants clicking it to ALSO randomise the token COLOUR and the GLYPH (plant icon) shown by default, so one tap gives a fresh name + look together.

CODE ANCHORS:
- The shuffle button: AddFlowView.swift:322 calls viewModel.reroll(entry); the button uses Image(systemName: "shuffle") (line 324) with accessibilityLabel "New random name" (line 328) — confirming it currently only changes the name. reroll(entry) lives on BasketAddViewModel (Sources/ViewModels/BasketAddViewModel.swift) and uses the injected RNG (RandomNicknameProvider / AnyRandomNumberGenerator, seedable for tests/DemoSeed).
- The GLYPH: each basket entry has a stored "var icon: PlantIcon" field (BasketAddViewModel.swift:36-43), defaulted via PlantIcon.default(forSpecies: species). The row already renders it through FixedGlyphPlantToken(icon: entry.icon, ...) at AddFlowView.swift:304 and even supports tapping it to change the icon ("Change icon for …", line 307). Randomising would pick a random case from PlantIcon's ~16 plant glyphs (Sources/DesignSystem/SproutIcon.swift:4-19: flower, leaf, plant, pottedPlant, cactus, flowerTulip, flowerLotus, grains, clover, treePalm, tree, treeEvergreen, acorn, cherries, carrot, pepper).
- The COLOUR: the token gradient is PlantTokenPalette.duo(for: entry.id) — i.e. currently DERIVED from the entry's UUID (AddFlowView.swift:304; PlantToken.swift PlantTokenPalette.duo(for:)). Per redesign-spec section 5 the token gradient is derived from the plant UUID with no stored colour field. So randomising the colour independently is a design question the convert sweep should settle: either re-derive by rerolling the entry's id/seed, or add an explicit stored palette/duo choice on the basket entry (and persist it onto the created Plant). Capturing this tension but NOT resolving it.

Should reuse the existing seedable RNG so DemoSeed/tests stay deterministic. Why it matters: the shuffle button reads as a full 'give me a different plant identity' control; only changing the name feels half-done, and the colour+glyph are the most visible part of the token.

title: Add Plants sheet title is off-centre (shared header)
description:
On the Add Plants sheet the header title "Add Plants" is not centred — it sits noticeably off to the RIGHT. (Voice-dictation typo in the report: "ad plans" = "Add Plants".)

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): the shared sheet header component SproutSheetHeader (Sources/DesignSystem/SheetScaffold.swift, struct at line 3) lays the bar out as an HStack (line 36): [Cancel button] (line 38) then Spacer (45) then Text(title) (47) then Spacer (51) then an optional confirm/trailing Button (54, onConfirm). The two Spacers centre the title BETWEEN the leading and trailing controls, not within the full width. When a sheet has a leading Cancel but NO trailing action button, the title is centred in the space to the right of Cancel — so it shifts right by roughly half the Cancel button's width, reading as "off to the right".

The Add Plants screen is exactly that case: AddFlowView (Sources/Views/AddFlowView.swift:217) constructs SproutSheetHeader(title: "Add Plants", onCancel: ...) with a leading Cancel but the confirm action is a full-width "Add N Plants" button pinned at the BOTTOM of the sheet (not a header trailing button), so the header's trailing slot is empty and the balancing element is missing.

This is a SHARED component, so the fix likely wants the title truly centred in the header's full width regardless of whether leading/trailing buttons exist (e.g. put the title in a ZStack/overlay centred across the whole bar, with the Cancel/confirm buttons floated left/right over it) — but that's a design choice for the convert sweep to settle, not resolved here. Other sheets that DO have both Cancel and a trailing action (e.g. Add Room: Cancel / Add) would look balanced today and must stay balanced after any fix.

Related: the ad-hoc header in the same file for the "Where do they live?" step (AddFlowView.swift:110-118) is a DIFFERENT hand-rolled HStack (Spacer / Text / Spacer / Cancel) and centres its title fine — only the SproutSheetHeader-based "Add Plants" step is affected, but the fix belongs in the shared SproutSheetHeader so every sheet benefits.

Why it matters: it's a visible polish defect on a primary sheet title; because it lives in the shared header, any current or future sheet with an empty trailing slot inherits the same off-centre look.

title: My Plants list token/glyph renders oversized
description:
On the My Plants list (the Plants screen), each plant row's glyph/token is rendering hugely oversized — the circular gradient plant token blows up to roughly HALF the card width, with a weird scalloped/notched edge, and it overlaps and sits behind the text (Stella / Monstera Adansonii / Every 6d shortened). It should be a small avatar on the left of the row, not a giant shape taking over the card.

INTENDED DESIGN (so this is a regression, not the spec): the list card is PlantCardView (Sources/Views/PlantListView.swift:318) — an HStack(spacing:14) of [token] [name/species/why VStack] [Spacer] [DueChip]. The token is PlantTokenView (Sources/Views/PlantListView.swift:413), which renders PlantToken(icon:duo:size:46, photo:) and additionally applies .frame(width:46, height:46) (lines 421-422). So the intended token is a fixed 46x46 pt circle. PlantToken itself (Sources/DesignSystem/PlantToken.swift:35) is a ZStack of a RadialGradient (or the photo) clipShaped to a Circle, with the glyph at .font(.system(size: size*0.45)), and .frame(width:size, height:size) (line 66) plus a drop shadow.

KEY CLUE: the SAME PlantToken renders correctly at small size elsewhere — e.g. the Home screen 'My Plants' bento tile shows the little overlapping tokens fine, and the empty-state uses PlantToken size 84 without issue. So the blow-up is specific to the My Plants LIST card context, which points at a layout/constraint problem in PlantCardView / PlantTokenView (e.g. the 46pt frame not actually constraining a RadialGradient — which has no intrinsic size and expands to fill — inside the row's HStack, possibly needing a .fixedSize or the frame applied differently), OR a stale-build artifact. Not resolving which here — flagging both as the investigation starting points.

Also visible: because the token overgrows, the name/species/'why' text gets overlapped and hard to read. The scalloped edge suggests either the Circle clip is fighting the expanded gradient or two tokens overlap.

Related redesign tasks: the My Plants list was restyled by T017 and the DueChip/token work by T018/T040. Why it matters: it's the primary plant list — the most-used screen — and the rows are currently visually broken/illegible.

title: My Plants: duplicate back buttons (one is a no-op)
description:
The My Plants page shows TWO back affordances: (1) the system navigation back button — the circular chevron top-left — because the screen is pushed onto HomeView's NavigationStack, and (2) a custom in-content "‹ Home" text button rendered just left of the "My Plants" title. User wants only ONE back control, chosen via proper iOS navigation practice. User explicitly asked that whoever implements this CONSULT THE AXIOM SKILLS (axiom-swiftui / axiom-apple-docs — the WWDC 2025+ SwiftUI navigation guidance) to pick the correct iOS 18/26 pattern rather than guessing.

EXTRA FINDING (important): the custom "‹ Home" button is not just redundant, it is BROKEN — its action is an empty stub. In Sources/Views/PlantListView.swift the header HStack (around line 66) has a Button whose action body is literally just a comment placeholder "/* dismiss or navigate back */" (line 68) and does nothing; its label is ChromeIcon.chevronLeft + Text("Home") in brand green (lines 70-78). So tapping "Home" today has NO effect — only the system back button actually pops to Home. Any fix should ensure the surviving control genuinely works (and supports the swipe-back gesture).

CODE ANCHORS / CONTEXT:
- Custom header + dead "Home" button: Sources/Views/PlantListView.swift, body starts ~line 64; the offending Button at 66-79; "My Plants" title at 81 (SproutFont.display(32)); the add FAB at 87-91.
- The screen is pushed by HomeView (Sources/Views/HomeView.swift) which owns the NavigationStack; HomeView hides its OWN root bar with .navigationBarHidden(true) but the pushed PlantListView does NOT hide the system back button, so it appears alongside the custom one. PlantListView never sets .navigationBarBackButtonHidden or a navigationTitle.
- The codebase already has two working patterns to align to: RoomDetailView (Sources/Views/RoomDetailView.swift, added in T045) uses a functional custom back Button via @Environment(\.dismiss) PLUS .navigationBarBackButtonHidden(true) so only one shows; PlantDetailView (Sources/Views/PlantDetailView.swift) uses a leading toolbar Label. RoomsView uses .navigationTitle("Rooms") with the default system back button. So navigation chrome is currently INCONSISTENT across pushed screens — this fix is a good moment to standardise (the redesign "‹ Home"/"‹ Rooms"/"‹ Plants" labeled-back style vs. the plain system back button).

DECISION LEFT FOR CONVERT/BUILD (not resolved here): either (a) drop the custom "Home" button and rely on the standard system back button (simplest, gets swipe-back for free, most idiomatic), or (b) keep the labeled "‹ Home" style for redesign consistency but wire it to dismiss AND hide the system back button (mirroring RoomDetailView). Whichever is chosen, apply it consistently across the pushed screens and confirm against Axiom's SwiftUI navigation guidance.

Why it matters: duplicate back buttons are confusing and off-pattern for iOS, and one of the two is currently a no-op — a visible correctness + polish issue on the primary plant list.

title: Plant detail broken: dupe back buttons + giant glyphs
description:
The individual plant detail page (Stella / Monstera Adansonii) is badly broken in TWO ways at once:

1) DUPLICATE BACK BUTTONS — two circular chevron buttons top-left. Same family as idea #6 (My Plants). Here it is PlantDetailView's leading toolbar Label("Plants", chevron.left) (Sources/Views/PlantDetailView.swift, toolbar ~line 70) rendering ALONGSIDE the system navigation back button (the screen is pushed onto the stack and PlantDetailView never sets .navigationBarBackButtonHidden). RoomDetailView (T045) already solved this correctly with a functional custom back Button + .navigationBarBackButtonHidden(true); plant detail was not fixed.

2) THE BOTTOM HALF IS TAKEN OVER BY TWO GIANT GLYPHS — a huge dark-green blob (an oversized droplet) on the left and an enormous tan/taupe pencil glyph filling most of the lower screen, with the real content (the schedule card, rhythm band, history — only a faint "...t due" is visible bottom-left) crushed to a sliver. The centered header (112pt PlantToken + "Stella" + italic species) renders fine at the top; it's the schedule card's inline icons that explode.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed, and likely SYSTEMIC — probably the same underlying bug as idea #5's oversized My Plants token): in PlantDetailView.scheduleCard the asset-catalog glyphs are sized with .font(.system(size:)) and have NO .resizable()/.frame() constraint:
- ChromeIcon.droplet.image .font(.system(size: 14, weight: .semibold)) at lines 154-155 (the green blob — tinted dueColor which is green for "Due in Nd").
- ChromeIcon.pencil.image .font(.system(size: 14)) at lines 171-172 (the tan pencil).
ChromeIcon.image is Image(rawValue).renderingMode(.template) over a RedesignIcons asset (Sources/DesignSystem/SproutIcon.swift). .font(.system(size:)) does NOT size a non-SF-Symbol template image — only .resizable() + .frame(width:height:) does. So wherever such a glyph has no enclosing fixed frame, it renders unbounded and balloons. The droplet at 154 sits in a ZStack with a Circle().frame(34) but the droplet itself is unframed, so it can outgrow the circle; the pencil at 171 is in an HStack with only a Spacer beside it, so nothing caps its size. The CORRECT pattern is used elsewhere, e.g. HomeView renders ChromeIcon.plus.image.resizable().aspectRatio(contentMode:.fit).frame(width:14,height:14) — glyphs sized that way render fine.

SUGGESTED (not resolving) INVESTIGATION for convert/build: grep the redesign for ChromeIcon/PlantIcon ".image" used with ".font(.system" but WITHOUT ".resizable()"/".frame(" and convert them to the resizable+frame pattern; that single fix likely resolves this screen's giant glyphs AND idea #5's oversized My Plants token. May also be partly masked/confused by stale builds, so reproduce-first.

CROSS-REFERENCES: strongly related to idea #5 (oversized token — same glyph-sizing family) and idea #6 (duplicate back buttons — same nav-chrome family). Convert should likely cluster #5/#6/#7 (and the shared-header/border polish ideas #1/#4) into a focused redesign-chrome fix batch.

Why it matters: this is the individual plant page — a core screen — and it is currently unusable/illegible, not just imperfect. Related redesign tasks: plant detail was T008 + restyled by T018 (rhythm band); DueChip/token by T040.

title: Guided-watering header title wraps to two lines
description:
On the guided-watering "report" screen (redesign screen 20 — the Close / "Water your plants" / Skip full-cover step), the centered header title "Water your plants" wraps onto TWO lines ("Water your" / "plants"). It should sit on a single line.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): in Sources/Views/GuidedWateringView.swift the header HStack (~line 40) lays out three items — Button("Close") with .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading) (lines 41-44), then Text("Water your plants") .font(SproutFont.display(17, weight: .bold)) (lines 46-47), then the trailing Button("Skip")/Spacer also with .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .trailing) (lines 51-57). Because BOTH flanking buttons take maxWidth: .infinity, they greedily expand and starve the middle Text of horizontal space, and the Text has NO .lineLimit(1) / .fixedSize(horizontal:) / .layoutPriority / .minimumScaleFactor, so it wraps. The title font size (17) is fine — this is width starvation, not an oversized font.

CONTEXT / anchors:
- Header code: Sources/Views/GuidedWateringView.swift:40-57. Same chrome is reused across the guided-watering steps (screens 20/21 report + action, and 22 done) so a fix here should keep those balanced.
- This is a DIFFERENT header component from the SproutSheetHeader in idea #4 (Add Plants off-centre title) — that one uses Spacers and a shared component; this one is a hand-rolled HStack with maxWidth:.infinity buttons — but they are the same CLASS of "flexible side controls squeezing/mis-centering the title" bug, so convert may want to handle them together and/or standardise header title behaviour (single line, truncation/scale rules) across all these headers.

Not resolving the fix here (e.g. lineLimit(1)+fixedSize on the title, or dropping maxWidth:.infinity on the buttons, or minimumScaleFactor) — that's for the convert/build step. Why it matters: a two-line title in a fixed-height header looks broken and misaligns the progress bar / hero beneath it on a core flow (guided watering).
